en.text: O Jagannāth, Lord of the universe
O Lord of yogamāyā, Lord of divine potency
I am the Āditya known as Viṣṇu
I alone am the eternal time flow
I am the month Agrahāyaṇ, the month of harvest (mid-November to mid-December)
I am the Supreme Absolute Truth, Svayam Bhagavān or the original Supreme Lord, the cause
who was born of the churning of nectar
of the predestined six transformations of the living beings
with the shining lamp of knowledge
in their divine relationship of servitude, friendship, etc
enlightening one another with the nectar of their devotional realisations
are now declaring this to me
(I am) the mantra prayer to Lord Indra
the four sages Sanaka, Sanandana, Sanat Kumār, and Sanātan, all known as brahmarṣis
Nārad Muni, the saint among the gods
sacrifice in the form of meditative chanting of the holy names
ability to discriminate between spirit and matter
forgiveness (or the seven wives of Dharma)
and the Supreme Lord of all beings
and of the great sages, too
(I am) the Vāyu known as Marīchi
Those who have dedicated their lives to Me
are born of My mind, which is manifest as Hiraṇyagarbha, Lord Brahmā
neither the demigods nor the demons
as the Supreme Brahma, directly the Supreme Lord
the supreme Śyāmasundar form, the supreme refuge
Of sanctifiers or of the rapid
equanimity, seeing the happiness and unhappiness of all beings as one’s own
situated within the heart of all beings
from all sins or obstacles to devotion
All activity in the universe of matter and spirit, and the Vedas and allied scriptures
of all causes, including Brahma, Paramātmā, and Bhagavān
Of all the mantras in the Sāma-veda
austerity as directed in the Vedas
and the fourteen Manus headed by Svayambhuva
to you, who are very dear to Me
the thunderbolt, the weapon of Lord Indra
the all-pervading Lord, the fountain-head of all opulences
to the expanse of My glories
(I am) Vāsuki, the king of snakes
the process of devotional union with You (bhakti-yoga)

ru.text: Гаруд̣а, носящий на себе Господа Вишну
молитвенное повторение Святых Имен Господа, обычно на четках
истины о Моем явлении и деяниях
из всех мантр в Са̄ма Веде
семеро (Марӣчи, Атри, Ан̇гирас, Пуластйа, Пулаха, Крату и Васиш̣т̣ха)
четверо вечно юных мальчиков-мудрецов Кума̄ров (Санака, Санандана, Санат-кума̄ра и Сана̄тана)
одним Своим частичным проявлением, как вездесущая Высшая Душа
